paper_title: Monosaccharide and Proteomic Analysis of T. crocea Shells

methods.description: Monosaccharide quantification of AIMs after bleaching steps using HPAE-PAD technology to analyze glucosamine and galactosamine.

steps.description: Lyophilized samples were hydrolyzed in 2 M trifluoroacetic acid at 105°C for 4 hours (100 µg/100 µL), and the solution was neutralized with sodium hydroxide.
steps.searchable_summary: hydrolyze samples in trifluoroacetic acid and neutralize with sodium hydroxide

steps.description: Filtered samples (20 µL) were eluted at 0.4 mL/min (35°C) using a sodium hydroxide gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: elute filtered samples using sodium hydroxide gradient

steps.description: External sugar and uronic acids standards were used for calibration (7 points per curve).
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate using external sugar and uronic acids standards

methods.description: Identification and classification of SMPs from T. crocea shells based on conserved domains, LCRs, and other characteristics.

steps.description: 39 SMPs were identified from the T. crocea shells, as shown by the Venn diagram.
steps.searchable_summary: identify SMPs from T. crocea shells

steps.description: Classified SMPs based on conserved domains, occurrence of LCRs, and absence of primary structure characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: classify SMPs based on domains and LCRs

steps.description: Sub-categorized conserved domains according to putative functions: affinity to polysaccharides, enzymatic activities, and protease inhibitors.
Identified SMPs with chitin-binding domains and other functional domains.
steps.searchable_summary: identify SMPs with chitin-binding and other domains

steps.description: Identified SMPs with enzymatic activity domains and protease inhibitor domains.
steps.searchable_summary: identify SMPs with enzymatic and inhibitor domains
outputs: SMPs with enzymatic and inhibitor domains

steps.description: Characterized LCR-containing proteins and their amino acid enrichment.
Predicted phosphorylation sites on S-rich and T-rich LCRs.
